content: Prompt Engineering 101: The foundation of working with AI. A prompt is an instruction given to an AI model. Effective prompts are specific, contextual, and iterative. The three elements of a strong prompt: Role (who the AI should be), Context (what situation), and Task (what to produce). Zero-shot prompting asks the model directly. Few-shot prompting provides examples first. Chain-of-thought prompting asks the model to reason step by step before answering. For founders: treat prompts like hiring briefs — the more clearly you define the role and outcome, the better the output.

Building AI-Powered Products: The MVP framework for AI products. Step 1 — Identify the one human problem your AI solves. Step 2 — Choose the simplest model that solves it (don't default to GPT-4 when GPT-3.5 works). Step 3 — Build a feedback loop into the product from day one. Step 4 — Measure outcomes, not outputs. Key principle: AI features should reduce friction, not add novelty. The best AI products feel invisible — the user feels smarter, not assisted. Paul Graham: 'Make something people want' applies doubly to AI — people want outcomes, not intelligence.

AI Tools for Founders — The Essential Stack: Research and synthesis — Perplexity, Claude. Writing and communication — Claude, Notion AI. Code generation — Cursor AI, GitHub Copilot. Image generation — Midjourney, DALL-E 3. Video — Runway, Kling. Audio — ElevenLabs, Suno. Automation — Zapier AI, Make. The principle: use AI to compress the distance between idea and execution. A solo founder with the right AI stack can move at the speed of a 10-person team. The skill is not knowing every tool — it is knowing which tool removes your specific bottleneck today.

Understanding Large Language Models for Founders: LLMs are trained on vast text corpora and learn statistical patterns of language. They predict the next token given a context window. Key concepts: Temperature controls randomness (0 = deterministic, 1+ = creative). Context window is the model's working memory. Fine-tuning adjusts model weights on your specific data. RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) injects external knowledge without retraining. For non-technical founders: think of an LLM as an extremely well-read collaborator who needs clear briefs and forgets everything between conversations. Your job is to give it context, constraints, and a clear goal.

AI Freelance Skills — The High-Value Stack for 2026: The skills commanding the highest rates in the AI economy: AI prompt engineering and system design ($80-200/hr). AI workflow automation (n8n, Make, Zapier AI) ($60-150/hr). Fine-tuning and RAG implementation ($100-250/hr). AI-assisted content strategy and creation ($50-100/hr). AI product management and scoping ($80-180/hr). The pattern: the highest earners combine domain expertise with AI execution. A healthcare professional who can build AI health tools earns 3x a pure AI generalist. Your existing knowledge is the moat — AI is the amplifier. Start with the intersection of what you already know and what AI can accelerate.
